jokes.text: I caught the tail end of what cinemax gets up to at night. Subscription canceled. I got that channel for eddie murphy movies, Not stimulation.
jokes.setup: Character mentions getting up early and catching something on TV
jokes.punchline: Reveals she accidentally saw adult content and her innocent reason for having the channel
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Shirley's innocent confusion about adult content while maintaining her wholesome persona. Well-structured with the misdirection.

jokes.text: Ah, well, I'll play a violin for them, And maybe she can strip to it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about ex-husband's new girlfriend who works at a club
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate but creative solution combining his violin with stripping
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce inappropriate response that's oddly creative. Character-specific but not exceptional.

jokes.text: This class is like a redhead that drinks scotch and loves die hard. I suggest you all get her number.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing pottery as a blow-off class
jokes.punchline: Specific, oddly romantic metaphor for an ideal situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff line - his specific taste and smooth delivery style. Highly quotable and original metaphor that perfectly captures his character.

jokes.text: Singing with tennille?
jokes.setup: Pierce asks them to guess which class he signed up for
jokes.punchline: Troy's mishearing/misunderstanding of 'Captain and Tennille'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Troy moment showing his youthful confusion with older references.

jokes.text: But the nearest body of water is 2 1/2 hours away. Leagues, abed. We don't measure water by hours.
jokes.setup: Troy points out logical flaw in sailing class location
jokes.punchline: Pierce's absurd correction using nautical terminology inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce moment showing his pretentious knowledge applied incorrectly. The logic is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Ha ha ha hee ha ha ho ho hey.
jokes.punchline: Pierce's bizarre, extended fake laugh that goes on too long
jokes.explanation: Pure Pierce awkwardness - the character integration is perfect but it's more uncomfortable than funny.

jokes.text: I was going to take a class on how to put together an online dating profile, but I think sailing in the parking lot is less pathetic.
jokes.punchline: Her assessment that parking lot sailing is less pathetic than online dating help
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment for Shirley - self-aware about her situation but with perfect comic timing on the comparative absurdity.

jokes.text: A black person on a sailboat? I gotta see this. I'm in.
jokes.punchline: Troy's self-aware comment about racial stereotypes in sailing
jokes.explanation: Good Troy character moment addressing stereotypes with his typical directness.

jokes.text: There's black people. Mm-hmm, there's two.
jokes.setup: Abed asking if he wants to sail with them
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact counting and Troy's confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed delivery - his analytical approach to social dynamics delivered with trademark deadpan. Very economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Hello, ladies. What are you guys making, Big balls here? Oh, yeah. You guys getting dirty?
jokes.setup: Jeff entering pottery class trying to be smooth
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempts at innuendo fall completely flat
jokes.explanation: Standard Jeff trying-too-hard moment. The cringe works for character but the jokes themselves are predictable.

jokes.text: Ladies, I don't know why you're laughing. I really do want to make my mom an ashtray.
jokes.punchline: Genuine, innocent explanation that contrasts with his earlier behavior
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection from his previous behavior to genuine sentiment. Works as character development.

jokes.text: Hello, my precious blueberries.
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly affectionate and odd nickname for students
jokes.explanation: Establishes the quirky pottery teacher character immediately. Memorable and specific.

jokes.text: I will tolerate no reenacting, whether it's ironic or sincere, of the patrick swayze, demi moore pottery scene in ghost.
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific rule about the most famous pottery scene in pop culture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke that acknowledges the obvious reference while setting up the episode's central gag. Very Community-specific humor.

jokes.text: This could be guy-on-girl, girl-on-guy... The hilarious guy-on-guy.
jokes.punchline: The clinical description ending with 'hilarious guy-on-guy'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the teacher's obsession with categorizing Ghost reenactments. The casual 'hilarious' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: If you so much as hum three notes from that righteous brothers song, with god is my witness, I will come at you. With everything I've got.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic escalation to threatening violence over humming
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the teacher's obsession to absurd threat levels. Well-structured buildup to threat.

jokes.text: I'm making falafel. Not edible.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact clarification that clay falafel isn't edible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - making food items from clay but needing to clarify they're not actually food. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: Look what I made. Participant badge.
jokes.setup: Jeff showing off his pottery work
jokes.punchline: His 'achievement' is making something that proves bare minimum effort
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff moment showing his minimal effort approach. The irony of being proud of a 'participation trophy' works well.

jokes.text: You look at me. you have a gift.
jokes.setup: Rich examining Jeff's accidental pottery success
jokes.punchline: Dramatic, intense delivery about pottery talent discovery
jokes.explanation: Sets up Rich's character as the pottery prodigy and Jeff's coming jealousy. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Jeff's competitive side had come out before. He had even displayed envy. But on that first day of pottery class, he discovered-- Abed. Yeah. What did we discuss? No voiceover. Sorry. It is kind of a crutch.
jokes.punchline: Jeff breaking the fourth wall to stop him, Abed's self-aware admission
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment that's quintessentially Community. Abed's self-awareness about narrative devices and Jeff's annoyance with it. Highly original and character-specific.

jokes.text: Welcome aboard the s. S. Nose candy, Recently purchased through government auction.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that it's a seized drug boat
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy moment - the casual delivery of using a former drug boat for sailing class. Very quotable boat name.

jokes.text: I commanded a jet ski through an electrical storm and only had one casualty. Jet skis only hold two people. Exactly. Saved half the crew.
jokes.setup: Pierce bragging about his nautical experience
jokes.punchline: His math makes it clear he killed his passenger but frames it as success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Pierce moment. The setup and punchline structure is perfect - his twisted logic about 'saving half the crew' when he killed one of two people. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, that's nice. It's not nice- it's your only weapon Against the raging sea.
jokes.setup: Shirley's polite response to the boat introduction
jokes.punchline: Slaughter's intense correction turning pleasantries into life-or-death drama
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Shirley's niceness vs. Slaughter's intensity. The escalation from 'nice' to 'weapon against the raging sea' is perfect.

jokes.text: Someone call a shrink. What? Well, you're usually the cool one.
jokes.setup: Britta commenting on Jeff's competitive behavior
jokes.punchline: Her casual observation that Jeff is losing his trademark coolness
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment highlighting Jeff's insecurity, but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Cat's pajamas? Okay, pierce.
jokes.punchline: Troy calling out the generational language slip
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's slip into Pierce-like language when flustered. Troy's callout is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Yes, I was robbed at the ymca. Again.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that this is a recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment - the casual 'again' suggests this happens regularly. Very economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: You're special. You're very special, jeff. You're the most special little boy in the world.
jokes.punchline: Overly intimate, childlike praise that makes everyone uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that establishes Rich's overly earnest character and makes Jeff uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I don't see students here. I see seamen. And I didn't create them. From the moment you climbed aboard, I saw seamen inside you. More importantly, you've stopped giggling At the word 'seamen.' And that's the mark of a real seaman.
jokes.punchline: Repeated use of 'seamen' with acknowledgment of the obvious double entendre
jokes.explanation: Classic juvenile humor elevated by the character's complete earnestness and meta-acknowledgment of the giggling.

jokes.text: Damn, he's ghost riding the jib.
jokes.punchline: Troy applying hip-hop terminology to sailing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Troy line combining his cultural background with nautical terminology. Very original and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: It landed on that hyundai. I mean, mermaid's car.
jokes.setup: Troy describing where Pierce's hat landed
jokes.punchline: His attempt to maintain nautical roleplay after breaking character
jokes.explanation: Great Troy moment showing his commitment to the bit even after breaking. The quick recovery is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You really screwed the pooch on that one, captain. and I can't swim.
jokes.punchline: Adding that he can't swim, making the sailing disaster worse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that adds to Pierce's complaints and references earlier swimming discussion.

jokes.text: But my medical degree does. I'm a doctor. A doctor? I just take pottery to unwind. Haven't lost a patient in five years. Puts a lot of pressure on a person.
jokes.setup: Jeff sarcastically saying pottery skill doesn't mean Rich can heal people
jokes.punchline: Rich casually revealing he's actually a doctor
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that reframes the entire character dynamic. The casual delivery of being a doctor is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: But I'll have to get a second opinion from a guy in my basket weaving class.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of another expert in another stereotypical 'easy' class
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the joke about professionals in hobby classes. Well-timed follow-up.

jokes.text: You know, a conman. A grifter. A ringer. He's an expert potter who signs up for novice classes to impress people.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his theory about Rich
jokes.punchline: His paranoid conspiracy theory about pottery class infiltration
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff paranoia moment. His conspiracy theory about pottery ringers is perfectly in character for his competitive nature.

jokes.text: Well, anyone could be a lawyer. You can even represent yourself. You can't do surgery on yourself. It's illegal. You'd get arrested. And then you'd get a free lawyer.
jokes.setup: Comparing law and medicine as impressive professions
jokes.punchline: Her logic spiraling into absurd legal scenarios
jokes.explanation: Excellent Britta logic spiral. Her pseudo-intellectual reasoning that circles back to free lawyers is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Rich just showed me how to make a flared lip on my pot and check for breast lumps.
jokes.setup: Annie describing her interaction with Rich
jokes.punchline: Casual combination of pottery instruction with medical advice
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of mundane pottery with serious medical advice. Annie's innocent delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I wanna kiss you on the mouth. I'm sorry?
jokes.setup: Jeff's response to Annie's pottery success
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate outburst and Annie's confused reaction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jeff's competitive jealousy leading to inappropriate comments. Annie's confusion sells it.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm being interrogated, but by someone who doesn't really want to know anything important.
jokes.punchline: Rich's perfect assessment of Jeff's investigative technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation that perfectly captures Jeff's paranoid but incompetent detective work. Very quotable and insightful.

jokes.text: Nobody gets out of santa fe without learning how to make a pot. 45% hispanic, Fifth highest native american population. They eat and breathe clay there.
jokes.setup: Jeff's conspiracy theory about Rich's background
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous cultural stereotyping and made-up statistics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jeff's paranoia into absurd cultural generalizations. The fake statistics and 'eat and breathe clay' is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: I think some of this research may have been done on wikipedia.
jokes.punchline: Britta's dry assessment of his source quality
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan deflation of Jeff's 'research.' The understatement works well to highlight his desperation.

jokes.text: Oh, jeff... You're goldbluming. I'm goldbluming? Heh, I, uh... I-I-I don't know what that means.
jokes.punchline: Jeff's denial while immediately demonstrating the Jeff Goldblum-like speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke that creates a new verb while having Jeff immediately demonstrate it. Perfect structure and execution. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Now I know what the 'c' in 'captain' stands for. crabapple.
jokes.punchline: His weak attempt at an insult using 'crabapple'
jokes.explanation: Decent Pierce moment showing his old-fashioned insult style. 'Crabapple' is appropriately weak for his character.

jokes.text: If the sea was always still and calm, nobody would respect her. I'm like the sea.
jokes.punchline: Her comparison of herself to the sea
jokes.explanation: Good Shirley character development showing her taking on authority. The sea metaphor is appropriately dramatic for her.

jokes.text: I hope I get multiple personalities. I get lonely in long showers.
jokes.setup: Discussion about mental health problems in college
jokes.punchline: Troy's specific hope for multiple personalities for companionship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy moment with perfectly absurd logic. The shower loneliness is oddly touching and very quotable. Excellent economy and originality.

jokes.text: Is that nautical talk or urban speak?
jokes.setup: Sailing commands being shouted during storm
jokes.punchline: Troy's confusion about whether it's sailing or street terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Troy observation about the similarity between sailing commands and street slang. Well-timed during the action.

jokes.text: I said we don't have time. What? No. Wha--wait. Pull me in. What do you mean 'time'? I--that's it. Wha-- No! Nooo!
jokes.punchline: Shirley's cold decision to abandon him, Pierce's growing horror
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy moment showing Shirley's ruthless captaining. Pierce's dawning realization is perfectly executed. Great escalation.

jokes.text: Get out of my parking spot, dip-wad! Security!
jokes.punchline: Reality intrudes with mundane parking complaint
jokes.explanation: Great reality check moment that punctures the sailing drama with mundane parking lot reality. Good structural timing.

jokes.text: Fatal mistake, richy boy. You...Are...Busted. I am? You just used the throwing off the hump method. That's an advanced pottery technique, which I know, because last night, I read the entire encyclopedia of pottery.
jokes.setup: Jeff thinking he's caught Rich in a mistake
jokes.punchline: His dramatic accusation based on obsessive research
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff obsession payoff. His dramatic 'busted' moment and the pottery encyclopedia research shows his competitive desperation.

jokes.text: Hey! No! What are you-- It's the hilarious guy-on-guy. One rule I asked you to follow. One.
jokes.setup: Jeff and Rich in pottery wheel situation
jokes.punchline: Teacher's horror at the exact thing he warned about
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the opening Ghost rule. Perfect setup and payoff structure. The teacher's horrified recognition is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: You failed a class so easy that people pass it in the hallway Get a contact credit!
jokes.setup: Jeff's frustration at being kicked out
jokes.punchline: His exaggerated claim about the class's easiness
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff meltdown moment. The 'contact credit' concept is funny and fits his lawyer-speak style.

jokes.text: ♪ oh ♪ ♪ my love ♪ ♪ my darlin' ♪ You son of a-- ♪ I've hungered for your touch ♪ No! Nooo! This is abuse! This is abuse! Stop it! That is a satanic sound!
jokes.setup: Someone humming the Righteous Brothers song
jokes.punchline: Teacher's complete breakdown at his worst nightmare
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and escalation. The teacher's complete psychological breakdown over his phobia is excellently executed. 'Satanic sound' is a great touch.

jokes.text: This is abuse! This is abuse! Stop it! That is a satanic sound!
jokes.setup: Rich singing 'Unchained Melody' to torment Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff's over-the-top reaction calling romantic song satanic
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff meltdown that shows his complete breakdown over pottery failure

jokes.text: Uh, they drowned me. Why? For a better grade.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking why Pierce isn't in sailing class
jokes.punchline: Pierce's matter-of-fact explanation of being murdered for grades
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with Pierce's casual acceptance of being murdered by his classmates. Perfect economy and delivery. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Jeffrey, when I was born, I got my umbilical cord wrapped around my neck, both arms, and one of my ankles. Mom said that there came a point when the doctors stopped delivering me and just started laughing.
jokes.punchline: His birth story ending with doctors laughing at the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Pierce backstory that's both touching and absurd. The escalation to doctors laughing is perfectly dark. Excellent character moment.

jokes.text: That thing some men call failure, I call living. Breakfast. And I'm not leaving till I've cleaned out the buffet.
jokes.punchline: His comparison of life to a breakfast buffet
jokes.explanation: Excellent Pierce philosophy with his typical food metaphor. The 'breakfast/living' connection is surprisingly poetic for him.

jokes.text: Don't need it. Never had it.
jokes.punchline: Pierce's matter-of-fact response about never having luck
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment showing his acceptance of his unlucky life. Very economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Man, that guy's life is one, big joke. I mean, I feel bad for him, but things have been running smoother since he drowned.
jokes.setup: Seeing Pierce struggling in water again
jokes.punchline: Troy's callous but honest assessment of the improvement
jokes.explanation: Good dark Troy moment balancing sympathy with practical assessment. The casual cruelty is well-executed.

jokes.text: Pierce is going to be the only person ever to drown in a parking lot. Twice.
jokes.setup: Pierce in trouble in the 'water' again
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of drowning in a parking lot, especially twice
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist observation. The specificity of 'twice' and 'parking lot' makes it perfectly ridiculous and quotable.

jokes.text: I am about to get the only 'a' I will ever get, and pierce isn't even dying.
jokes.punchline: Troy's selfish academic priorities versus Pierce's life
jokes.explanation: Good Troy character moment showing his priorities. The casual dismissal of Pierce's danger is darkly funny.

jokes.text: The sea may be cold and unforgiving, but I'm not. The ship might go down, but, uh... At least she'll go down with honor.
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic maritime philosophy despite being in a parking lot
jokes.explanation: Good Shirley character growth moment. Her embrace of nautical drama is well-executed, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: Jeff, you're a normal person. There's nothing very special about you at all. You're going to be great at a few things, but really crappy at many more. And that takes a lot of the pressure off, so you can live a full, happy life.
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment about Jeff's mediocrity being liberating
jokes.explanation: Excellent wisdom disguised as insult. Rich's advice is actually profound while being completely deflating to Jeff's ego.

jokes.text: Jeff, you're a normal person. There's nothing very special about you at all. You're going to be great at a few things, But really crappy at many more.
jokes.punchline: The brutally honest but ultimately kind assessment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical encouragement. Rich's honesty is both harsh and liberating, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And it was only in your imagination. My bad. Kind of a sloppy mom.
jokes.setup: Jeff having imaginary conversation with his mother
jokes.punchline: His imaginary mom being self-aware about being imaginary and sloppy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Jeff's imagination breaks the fourth wall. The 'sloppy mom' self-assessment is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: A clay ashtray. You're an idiot. Why do you have time to make pottery? Couldn't you use a little more practice being a doctor?
jokes.setup: Jeff's mom commenting on his pottery
jokes.punchline: Her escalating criticism even in his own fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jeff can't even imagine unconditional approval. The mom confusing Rich and Jeff is perfect.

jokes.text: Why do you have time to make pottery? Couldn't you use a little more practice being a doctor? Not that it'll ever bring your brother back to life. It was supposed to be you on that roller coaster, richard. It was supposed to be you!
jokes.punchline: His imaginary mom's brutal guilt trip about his dead brother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation that explains Rich's perfectionism through imaginary parental trauma. The roller coaster reveal is perfectly horrific and funny.
